sources.documentName: City of Cerritos Combined Financial Program FY 2025-2026 (Adopted Budget)
sources.description: The officially adopted annual budget for the City of Cerritos, a 370+ page document detailing all revenue sources, departmental expenditures, capital improvements, and fund statements for the fiscal year beginning July 1, 2025.

sources.documentName: City of Cerritos FY 2025-26 Budget at a Glance
sources.description: One-page infographic summarizing FY 2025-26 citywide operating expenses ($131.4M), General Fund revenue ($101.5M), and capital improvement plan ($23.0M).
Interactive portal providing 10-year historical expenditure and revenue data by department and fund. Data spans FY 2016-17 through FY 2025-26 (Adopted).

sources.documentName: City of Cerritos Combined Financial Program FY 2024-2025 (Adopted Budget)
sources.description: Prior year adopted budget used for year-over-year comparison and trend analysis.

sources.documentName: City of Cerritos — Current Green Efforts
sources.description: Official city sustainability page listing active environmental programs including recycled water, solar energy, CNG fleet, LED traffic signals, and urban forestry.

sources.documentName: City of Cerritos — Recycled Water Program
sources.description: Details on Cerritos's recycled water system operating since 1988: 25 miles of distribution lines, 200+ acres irrigated, 722 million gallons of potable water conserved annually.

sources.documentName: U.S. Census Bureau — Cerritos City, California
sources.description: Population estimates and demographic data for per-capita calculations. Cerritos population: 51,460 (2020 Census).
City Council adopted strategic plan outlining 7 goals for city operations, including Goal 5 (Technology), Goal 6 (Fiscal Transparency & Sustainability), and Goal 7 (Community Communications).

sources.documentName: Metropolitan Water District of Southern California — Rates & Charges
sources.description: MWD wholesale water rates used as benchmark for recycled water cost savings calculations. Tier 1 treated water rate approximately $0.005/gallon.

sources.documentName: California Air Resources Board — GHG Emission Inventory
sources.description: CARB grid emissions factor for California electricity: 622 lbs CO2e per MWh. Used for energy literacy emissions calculations and renewable energy scenario modeling.

Athens Services operates Cerritos residential waste collection with a 3-cart system (blue recycling, green organics, black landfill). All waste vehicles are CNG-operated. Used for recycling quiz content and SB 1383 education.

sources.documentName: EPA eGRID — Emissions & Generation Resource Integrated Database
sources.description: National average grid emissions factor: 852 lbs CO2e per MWh. Used alongside CARB data for California vs. national emissions comparison in energy literacy module.
